# @zakkster/lite-headless/menu

createMenu(options) -> MenuHandle

WAI-ARIA menu primitive. Real DOM focus on items with roving tabindex.
Designed for menubars, action menus, dropdown menus, and nested submenus.

## Why real focus (vs combobox's aria-activedescendant)

Menu items are actions, not selections. Screen readers should announce
"focused on Save" when keyboard nav lands on an item. Real DOM focus does
this naturally. The aria-activedescendant pattern is for input elements
that need to retain focus while a separate "active" cursor moves.

## Options

- open: WriteSignal<boolean>           -- external controlled mode
- defaultOpen: boolean = false
- onOpenChange(next, reason)           -- 'trigger'|'api'|'escape'|'outside'|'select'|'tab'|'back'

- placement: string = 'bottom-start'
- offset: number = 4
- flip: boolean = true
- shift: boolean = true
- boundary: 'clipping'|'viewport'|HTMLElement = 'clipping'

- typeahead: boolean = true
- typeaheadTimeout: number = 500       -- ms before buffer resets
- loop: boolean = true                  -- ArrowDown at end wraps to start
- closeOnSelect: boolean = true        -- close after item activation
- closeOnEscape: boolean = true
- closeOnOutsideClick: boolean = true

- isSubmenu: boolean = false           -- when true: ArrowLeft/Escape closes
                                          THIS menu only, not the chain
- submenuOpenDelay: number = 100       -- ms before hover opens a submenu
- submenuCloseDelay: number = 300      -- ms grace before closing on leave;
                                          also covers pointer crossing the gap

- container: HTMLElement|null = document.body
- transition: boolean = false

## Returned

{
    open: ReadSignal<boolean>,
    status: ReadSignal<'closed'|'opening'|'open'|'closing'>,
    setOpen(value, reason),
    toggle(),
    attachTrigger(buttonEl),
    attachAnchor(el),                  -- separate positioning anchor from trigger
    attachContextTarget(el),           -- v0.4: right-click on el opens at pointer
    attachMenu(ulEl),                  -- gets role="menu"
    attachItem(liEl, { onSelect, disabled, label }),
    attachCheckboxItem(liEl, { checked, onCheckedChange, label, disabled }),  -- v0.4
    attachRadioItem(liEl, { value, group, onValueChange, label, disabled }),   -- v0.4
    attachSeparator(el),               -- gets role="separator"
    attachSubmenu(parentItemEl, submenuInstance),
    attachInside(el),                  -- mark external el as inside for outside-click
    destroy(),
    destroyed
}

## Keyboard model

When CLOSED, on trigger:
  ArrowDown / Enter / Space            open + focus first item
  ArrowUp                              open + focus last item
  <printable char>                     open + typeahead

When OPEN, on menu (focus is on an item):
  ArrowDown / ArrowUp                  move focus (loops by default, skips disabled)
  Home / End                           first / last focusable
  Enter / Space                        activate (calls onSelect, closes if closeOnSelect)
  ArrowRight                           open submenu on focused parent (if any)
  ArrowLeft                            close THIS menu (only when isSubmenu:true)
  Escape                               close menu
  Tab                                  close menu (Tab default action moves focus on)
  <printable char>                     typeahead (same-char cycles; mixed accumulates)

## Submenu pattern

```js
const file = createMenu({ placement: "bottom-start" });
file.attachTrigger(fileBtn);
file.attachMenu(fileMenuEl);
file.attachItem(newItem,    { onSelect: () => doNew() });
file.attachItem(recentItem, { label: "Recent…" });

const recent = createMenu({ placement: "right-start", isSubmenu: true });
recent.attachMenu(recentMenuEl);
recent.attachItem(...);

file.attachSubmenu(recentItem, recent);
// internally:
//   recent.attachAnchor(recentItem)         positioning
//   parent.aria-haspopup="menu" on recentItem
//   parent.aria-expanded synced to recent.open
//   hover open/close with delays
//   ArrowRight on recentItem when focused opens recent + focuses its first item
```

Each submenu is its own createMenu instance, so arbitrary nesting works. Only
one submenu per level is open at a time (entering a sibling closes the previous).

## Slot conventions (for <lite-menu>)

- [data-trigger]               -- the button (gets aria-haspopup="menu")
- [data-menu]                  -- the ul/ol (gets role="menu")
- [data-item][data-on-select=key] -- menu item; emits CustomEvent('select')
                                     with detail={key, el} on the host
- [data-disabled] on item       -- aria-disabled, skipped in nav
- [data-separator]              -- separator element (gets role="separator")

## States exposed on items

- role="menuitem" | "menuitemcheckbox" | "menuitemradio"
- tabindex: "0" on the currently-focused item, "-1" on others (roving)
- data-focused: present on the focused item (CSS hook)
- aria-disabled: "true" on disabled items
- data-disabled: present on disabled items (CSS hook)
- aria-haspopup="menu" + aria-expanded on parent items with submenus
- aria-checked + data-checked: on checkbox/radio items

## v0.4 additions

- **Context menu mode** (`attachContextTarget`): right-clicking the target
  preventDefaults the native menu and opens this menu positioned against a
  0×0 virtual anchor at the pointer location.
- **`attachCheckboxItem`**: sticky toggle, doesn't close on activation.
- **`attachRadioItem`**: group-scoped single selection, closes on activation.
- **Safe-triangle** (`safeTriangle: true` option, default on): pointer
  crossing from parent item toward submenu doesn't trigger close as long
  as the pointer stays inside the convex region between leave-point and
  submenu's near edge. Falls back to `submenuCloseDelay` timer at the
  hard cap (`2 × submenuCloseDelay`).

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
